facts: If you fell into a black hole, you'd experience gravitational forces 1 billion times stronger than Earth's, stretching you like putty over a distance of about 1 million kilometers, roughly the length of 200,000 NYC marathons
The point of no return, called the event horizon, would be around 12 miles from a stellar-mass black hole, which is roughly the size of Texas
As you approached the singularity, time would slow down relative to Earth time, and 1 minute for you would equal about 700 years on our planet
idea.payoff: and we still don't know what happens next
idea.title: Black Hole Survival 🚀
idea.description: Imagine being pulled into a cosmic abyss 🌌, where gravity is so strong it warps time and space. You're about to experience the most extreme environment in the universe 🌟. But what really happens when you cross the point of no return 🤔?

scenes.visual: Abstract, swirling dark vortex representing a black hole, with subtle gravitational lensing effects. Stars visible in the distant background.

scenes.text: Gravitational force: 1 BILLION x Earth's! You'd stretch 1 Million KM
scenes.visual: Stylized human silhouette being pulled towards the black hole, visibly elongating into a spaghetti-like strand. Numbers (1 Billion, 1 Million) subtly appearing and emphasizing.

scenes.text: The Event Horizon: Point of NO return. ~12 Miles (Size of Texas)
scenes.visual: Close-up of a black hole's event horizon, depicted as a sharp, glowing ring. A stylized map outline of Texas appears and briefly pulses within the ring to show scale.

scenes.text: Time Dilation: 1 Minute for you = 700 Years on Earth!
scenes.visual: Two clocks side-by-side; one rapidly spinning its hands, the other barely moving. The background subtly distorts and warps around the 'fast' clock.

scenes.text: AND WE STILL DON'T KNOW WHAT HAPPENS NEXT.
scenes.visual: The black hole visual slowly fades or transforms into a vast, nebulous, and mysterious cosmic expanse, with a subtle, shimmering question mark appearing in the distance.
